title: Frontend — auth context, routing, and protected routes
description: Set up React Router with public routes (login, signup) and protected routes (dashboard, account). Create an AuthContext that provides the current user state and login/logout functions throughout the app.
acceptanceCriteria: Unauthenticated users visiting /dashboard are redirected to /login
Authenticated users visiting /login or /signup are redirected to /dashboard
AuthContext provides: user, isLoading, login(), logout() to all components
login() stores token in localStorage and sets user state
logout() clears token from localStorage and redirects to /login
On app load, if a token exists in localStorage, the user is fetched and set in context
App shows a loading state while checking auth on initial load

steps.details: Create src/web/src/context/AuthContext.tsx. Define AuthContextType interface with: user (User | null), isLoading (boolean), login(token: string, user: User): void, logout(): void. Create context with createContext. Create AuthProvider component that: reads token from localStorage on mount, if token exists calls getMe() to load user (sets isLoading true/false), exposes login() which stores token + sets user, exposes logout() which removes token, resets user, navigates to /login.

Create src/web/src/components/ProtectedRoute.tsx. Component checks isLoading — if loading, render a centered spinner. If not loading and no user, return <Navigate to='/login' replace />. Otherwise render <Outlet />.

steps.description: Set up React Router in App.tsx
steps.details: In src/web/src/App.tsx, wrap the app in BrowserRouter and AuthProvider. Define routes: / redirects to /dashboard, public routes: /login, /signup. Protected routes (wrapped in ProtectedRoute): /dashboard, /account. Each route renders the appropriate page component (create placeholder components if pages don't exist yet).

Start dev server. Visit /dashboard without being logged in — verify redirect to /login. After login, visit /login — verify redirect to /dashboard. Refresh on /dashboard while logged in — verify user stays on /dashboard (auth persists via localStorage).

technicalNotes: Use useNavigate() from react-router-dom for programmatic navigation inside components
The isLoading state prevents a flash of the login page before the stored token is validated
Use Outlet from react-router-dom inside ProtectedRoute to render child routes
